conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex, who is 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand his current situation.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he enrolled in full-time non-advanced education such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or an approved training program?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and hasn't enrolled in anything since.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I need to gather a bit more information about your relationship with Alex and your living situation.

**Do you live in the UK, and is Alex your biological child, adopted child, or a child you're responsible for in another way?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. Alex is my biological child and he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Before I can make a final determination on your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the official guidance I've reviewed, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

According to the rules:
- Child Benefit is available for children under 16
- For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 **and** they stay in approved education or training

Since Alex is 18 years old and is **not** currently in approved education or training (he finished school and hasn't enrolled in anything since), unfortunately you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but based on your circumstances, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

The reason is that Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. While Child Benefit continues for children aged 16-19, this is only if they remain in approved education or training (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved training programs).

However, if Alex decides to enroll in approved education or training in the future, you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit again at that point. If his circumstances change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office to discuss your options.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and has finished school. He is not currently enrolled in any approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, while children under 16 automatically qualify, for children aged 16 or over (up to age 20), they must be staying in approved education or training for the claimant to remain eligible. Since Alex is not in approved education or training, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for him.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old biological son Alex. While they meet the residency and responsibility requirements, Alex does not meet the age criteria as he is over 16 and not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and has finished school. He is not currently enrolled in any approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, while children under 16 automatically qualify, for children aged 16 or over (up to age 20), they must be staying in approved education or training for the claimant to remain eligible. Since Alex is not in approved education or training, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for him.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old biological son Alex. While they meet the residency and responsibility requirements, Alex does not meet the age criteria as he is over 16 and not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
